commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

issues.description: Search filtering causes non-matching elements to disappear completely, which breaks the visual integrity of the periodic table grid.
issues.fix: Instead of removing elements, dim the opacity of non-matching elements to maintain the periodic table's structure.

issues.description: Grid cells lack semantic structure (e.g., role='button') and keyboard focus states are reliant on default card styling.
issues.fix: Ensure the Clickable component applies proper ARIA attributes and focus styles; wrap the grid in a nav or main landmark.

issues.description: The gap and aspect ratio are good, but the lack of labels for grid axes makes it harder to interpret.
issues.fix: Consider adding labels for row/column groups if the design allows, or ensure the legend effectively compensates.

evaluations.critique: The component is clean and well-structured, utilizing the grid effectively for a complex layout. To reach production quality, it needs to preserve grid integrity during filtering and include the requested legend to improve user orientation.

issues.description: Missing the required detail panel for selected elements.
issues.fix: Implement a detail view section that renders when an element is clicked to show name, weight, and category.

issues.description: Missing the required color-coded legend.
issues.fix: Add a legend component below or above the grid that displays the categories with their corresponding CSS variable colors.

issues.description: The periodic table layout is incorrect; it is not using the standard 18-column grid format (the inner transition/lanthanide gaps are handled incorrectly).
issues.fix: Map the grid-column and grid-row values precisely to an 18-column grid system to reflect the standard IUPAC layout.

issues.description: Elements lack the requested color-coding based on chemical category.
issues.fix: Apply background colors to the element tiles using CSS variables mapped to the 'category' property of the data.

issues.description: The grid of cells lacks semantic structure and labels. Clicking a Card without ARIA attributes makes it difficult for screen readers to interpret.

issues.description: Grid layout on mobile devices will likely break as 18 columns are too narrow for small viewports.

commit.name: E-Commerce Product Page
commit.description: Product detail page with image, tabs, quantity selector, and cart
commit.prompt:
Build an e-commerce product detail page. Layout:

Top section:
- Product image placeholder (use a colored box with the product name)
- Product title, price (with currency from props), and rating stars (1-5)
- Stock status badge ("In Stock" green or "Low Stock" warning)

Middle section — Tab navigation with 3 tabs:
- "Description" tab: renders product description text from props
- "Specifications" tab: renders key-value pairs from props as a table
- "Reviews" tab: renders a list of reviews (author, rating, text, date) from props

Bottom section:
- Quantity selector (increment/decrement buttons, min 1, max from stock)
- "Add to Cart" button (disabled if out of stock)
- Call props.onAddToCart with { productId, quantity } when clicked

Requirements:
- All product data from props — nothing hardcoded
- Use design system tokens for all colors, spacing, typography
- Price should format with currency symbol from props
- Rating stars should be visual (filled/empty)
- Responsive: stack image above details on mobile widths

product.description: Product object with id, name, price, currency, rating, stock, description, specifications[], reviews[]

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

issues.description: Form inputs lack explicit aria-invalid or error messaging when email validation fails.
issues.fix: Add an error message display below the email input when !isValidEmail and the user has touched the field.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured, clean, and fulfills all functional requirements effectively. It utilizes the design system components correctly and provides a solid base for a wizard pattern; minor enhancements to step transitions and inline error validation would make it truly production-grade.

issues.description: The layout is extremely narrow, leaving the majority of the screen as wasted whitespace.
issues.fix: Increase the width of the form container and center it properly within the viewport.

issues.description: The 'Back' button and 'Next' button are disproportionately spaced, and the avatar selection group is poorly aligned/spaced.
issues.fix: Use a Flexbox layout for the footer actions to justify them properly (e.g., space-between) and improve padding around form elements.

issues.description: Visual style is very basic; lacks proper input styling, clear labels, and card-based grouping.
issues.fix: Implement a card container for the form, style inputs with better borders/focus states, and improve the typography scale.

evaluations.critique: The component functions as a basic structure but lacks the visual refinement and layout planning expected of a professional wizard. The extreme whitespace and poor alignment make it look unfinished; it needs better grouping of elements and more intentional spacing to improve usability.

issues.description: The email input lacks real-time validation error feedback when the format is incorrect.
issues.fix: Add a conditionally rendered helper text or error message within the FormField when the email is invalid.

issues.description: Progress bar lacks an aria-valuenow label for screen readers.
issues.fix: Add accessibility attributes to the Progress component or ensure it has a proper label/role.

issues.description: The review screen list is slightly cramped; lacks subtle separators between fields.
issues.fix: Use a Divider component or increased padding/border between row items in the summary box.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured and follows the design system primitives effectively. The logic is clean, and the step navigation behaves as expected. Adding minor validation feedback and accessibility labels would elevate it to a production-ready standard.

issues.description: The layout is extremely narrow, centered at the very top of the screen with excessive whitespace below, looking unfinished.
issues.fix: Use a container component with a max-width, center the content vertically or provide a cohesive card-based wrapper to hold the form elements.

issues.description: The avatar selection uses raw emojis which look unpolished and inconsistent with professional UI design.
issues.fix: Use styled buttons, SVG icons, or images for the avatar selection to create a more consistent visual style.

issues.description: The UI lacks a cohesive card wrapper, making it look like a collection of floating inputs rather than a structured onboarding wizard step.
issues.fix: Group the form fields within a well-padded card with subtle border or shadow to define the wizard's step area.

evaluations.critique: The component effectively captures the required fields for Step 1, but the visual execution lacks the polish of a professional onboarding wizard. The layout is disjointed, and the lack of a card container makes the form feel floating and unanchored. Better use of spacing, container styling, and replacing raw emojis with consistent graphical elements would significantly improve the UX.

issues.description: The validation error message for the email field in Step 1 persists even when the field is empty until the user types, potentially confusing users who haven't started interacting yet.
issues.fix: Use a local state or check for `isDirty` before showing the validation error message.

issues.description: The progress bar lacks an `aria-valuenow`, `aria-valuemin`, and `aria-valuemax` attribute to convey progress to screen readers.
issues.fix: Ensure the underlying Progress component or its wrapper receives appropriate ARIA attributes based on the current step percentage.

evaluations.critique: This is a high-quality, production-ready onboarding wizard. It effectively uses design tokens and layout primitives to create a clean, professional user interface. The logic is well-structured and handles the transition between steps gracefully.

issues.description: The layout is extremely narrow, leaving excessive white space on the sides. The input fields are unnecessarily long compared to the content.
issues.fix: Constrain the width of the form container (e.g., max-width: 600px) and center it within the viewport using margin: auto or flexbox alignment.

issues.description: The avatar selection is poorly spaced, and the radio buttons lack visual alignment with the icons.
issues.fix: Use a grid or flexbox with consistent gap spacing for the avatar radio group, and perhaps replace raw emojis with properly styled graphical representations.

issues.description: Only Step 1 is visible, and the UI lacks clear structure for navigating to subsequent steps or indicating the total step count clearly.
issues.fix: Ensure the progress bar reflects the 3-step count (e.g., 'Step 1 of 3') and ensure the layout is ready to swap content for subsequent steps.

evaluations.critique: The component successfully renders the core elements for Step 1, but the layout feels unfinished and unconstrained, causing it to stretch awkwardly across the screen. It lacks the polish of a professional onboarding wizard and needs better spacing, centering, and refined typography to meet design system standards.

issues.description: Lack of visual transition effects between steps; the content snaps immediately without animation.
issues.fix: Wrap the step content in a CSS transition or a library component that supports enter/exit animations.

issues.description: The review screen summary uses plain text elements rather than a descriptive definition list or labeled group for screen readers.
issues.fix: Use a proper list structure (ul/li) or fieldset/legend for the summary section to improve structural clarity.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured, follows all design token requirements, and implements the requested workflow accurately. The state management is clean and the UI layout is highly professional and consistent with modern standards.

issues.description: The component only shows the first step of the requested 3-step wizard.

issues.description: The form elements are very narrow and lack proper container max-width or centering context.
issues.fix: Wrap the form in a card container with a maximum width and adequate padding to create a better visual anchor.

issues.description: The avatar selector looks disjointed with icons placed immediately next to radio buttons without labels or clear grouping.
issues.fix: Use a grid layout for avatar options with larger, selectable touch targets.

evaluations.critique: The component successfully renders the initial step of the onboarding process, but it fails to meet the completeness requirements as it lacks the subsequent steps and state management. issues.description: Lack of visual transition animation between steps.

issues.description: The email error message logic in step 1 triggers before the user has finished typing, which can be disruptive.
issues.fix: Implement a 'touched' state for inputs so that validation errors only appear after the user has interacted with the field and blurred/left it.

evaluations.critique: The component is highly professional and implements all business requirements accurately.
